REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF CIVIL PENALTIES AND FINDING OF CIVIL CONTEMPT FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANTS' GOOD-FAITH EFFORTS TO COMPLY, COUPLED WITH DELAYS CAUSED BY THIRD-PARTY VENDORS, NEGATES THE WILLFULNESS ELEMENT FOR A CONTEMPT FINDING. THE COURT SUGGESTS THE PARTIES ATTEMPT TO EXPLORE NEGOTIATION OF THE FEES AND COSTS ASSOCIATED WITH THE VACANCY NOVS IF POSSIBLE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). VISITING JUDGE: DENNIS HAYASHI; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
Motion For Imposition Of Civil Penalties And Finding Of Civil Contempt For Failure To Comply With Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar May-29-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA Motion For Imposition Of Civil Penalties And Finding Of Civil Contempt For Failure To Comply With Preliminary Injunction

is DENIED without prejudice. Defendants' good-faith efforts to comply, coupled with delays caused by third-party vendors, negates the willfulness element for a contempt finding. The Court suggests the parties attempt to explore negotiation of the fees and costs associated with the Vacancy NOVs if possible.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Visiting Judge: Dennis Hayashi; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)
